evals.prompt: Create a background job for processing uploaded CSV files. It should parse the CSV, validate rows, and store results. Need progress tracking.
evals.expected_output: Handler in worker, enqueue endpoint in backend, frontend progress hook
expectations: Handler created at services/worker/app/handlers/ with async function signature (payload, on_progress)
Handler registered in handlers/__init__.py HANDLERS dict
Backend service uses JobQueue from packages.redis.queue to create_job
Progress callbacks report meaningful percentages and messages
Frontend uses useJobStream hook for real-time progress

evals.prompt: Add a background job that sends welcome emails after user signup. Payload includes user_id and email address.
evals.expected_output: Simple fire-and-forget job with handler and enqueue logic
expectations: Handler function takes payload dict with user_id and email fields
Uses packages.logging.get_logger, not print or logging.getLogger
Backend enqueue uses ServiceUnavailableError when Redis not configured
Return dict from handler includes status field
Handler registered with descriptive job_type key
